title: Avoid overriding LDFLAGS in Makefile
body:
Similar to https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2752:

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

`LDFLAGS` is a common/standard variable to hold linker flags for C/C++ compilers.
This PR uses a different name (`SKOPEO_LDFLAGS`) for the `-ldflags` passed on to Go so that we avoid overriding `LDFLAGS` set in the environment.

@mtrmac in https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1099#pullrequestreview-525574335 :
> For the record, a missing part of the motivation is that CGo uses LDFLAGS when referring to C libraries, so this breaks overriding e.g. paths with `-L`.

comments.body:
> For the record, a missing part of the motivation is that CGo uses LDFLAGS when referring to C libraries, so this breaks overriding e.g. paths with `-L`.

Indeed. I should've added such an explanatory sentence here too, but forgot to. Thanks.

> Grepping the source, does `default.nix` need updating? I have no idea what that thing even is.

Ah, good catch! I could make a followup PR for that -- but that line looks non-functional anyway: it does `export LDFLAGS=...` but the `Makefile` has `LDFLAGS :=...` and `make` isn't invoked with `LDFLAGS=...` or `-e` (plus, even it were to override the `Makefile`'s `LDFLAGS`, it would then miss the `-ldflags` for `EXTRA_LDFLAGS`).
Let's just ask the author: @hswong3i, what is the intent of the `export LDFLAGS` line in the `nix/default.nix` file?
`buildah` and `podman` have the same putatively non-functional `export LDFLAGS=...` line.

> Ah, good catch! I could make a followup PR for that -- but that line looks non-functional anyway: it does `export LDFLAGS=...` but the `Makefile` has `LDFLAGS :=...` and `make` isn't invoked with `LDFLAGS=...` or `-e` (plus, even it were to override the `Makefile`'s `LDFLAGS`, it would then miss the `-ldflags` for `EXTRA_LDFLAGS`).
> Let's just ask the author: @hswong3i, what is the intent of the `export LDFLAGS` line in the `nix/default.nix` file?

For your reference:
  - https://github.com/containers/crun/blob/master/nix/default.nix
  - https://github.com/containers/conmon/blob/master/nix/default.nix
  - https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/master/nix/default.nix
  - https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/nix/default.nix
  - https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/nix/default.nix
  - https://github.com/cri-o/cri-o/blob/master/nix/default.nix

Here I share as much as similar skeleton across repos, so when running `nix build -f nix/` locally they could share as much of complied package result as possible, therefore reduce overall compile duration.

Hopefully we could also synchronize similar changes on `CFLAGS`/`LDFLAGS`/`EXTRA_LDFLAGS`/`BUILDTAGS`/etc across above repos: a simple stupid implementation could give us more intention for maintenance, daily ;-)

From that I (kind of) gather that the `LDFLAGS` in there is indeed meant to be just the "normal C" `LDFLAGS` -- which is good, in general and because it means this PR was beneficial for your intentions too.
The thing that threw me off a bit is the `-w` flag in there -- that one is rather uncommon to see (in `LDFLAGS` and overall you rather encounter `-W...` flags in the wild).

CI failure seems unrelated (maybe connection issue?).
https://travis-ci.org/github/containers/skopeo/jobs/741944968#L2178

Running skopeo copy docker://estesp/busybox:latest dir:/tmp/copy-manifest-list-storage-dir703840111

    assertSkopeoSucceeds(c, "", "copy", "docker://estesp/busybox:latest", "dir:"+dir1)

Copying blob sha256:57c14dd66db0390dbf6da578421c077f6de8e88edd0815b4caa94607ba5f4c09

@mtrmac @mbargull @hswong3i So this is ready to merge?
@rhatdan, yes, good to go! (The `LDFLAGS` usage in `nix/default.nix` is untouched/unimpeded by this PR.)
